lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Don't waste my time trying to change settings on a thing you haven't bought yet!

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Eyup. They call me Skully. I run this Wilderness Loot Chest. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Well, you know how when you kill someone all of their stuff just ends up in a pile on the floor and it's really hard to pick it all up quickly?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I've never killed anyone else.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: ...Oh. Well, when you do, all of their stuff just ends up in a pile on the floor and it's really hard to pick it all up quickly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: So, I made an enchantment that teleports the loot to another dimension instead!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How does that help?

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Well, instead of getting a pile of rubbish, you get a single key. You just take the key to this chest and BAM! There's your loot!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So the loot is safe in this other dimension?

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Eh, seems like it. As long as they've had the key, I've never had anyone unable to get the stuff back out. Though if you lose the key, you can't get the stuff out.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Oh, and be careful running about with too many keys on you. Other people will know you've got them, and I'm not gonna stop them opening the chest!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So if I kill someone and take their keys, I can use them myself?

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: ...Eh, if you wanna think about it that way, sure.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: I got nothing better to do, so I'll keep track of the value of the stuff you unlock.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: And anything you don't want, you can have the chest destroy it.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Except the food. I'll eat that instead.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Now, if you want your own keys instead of relying on the misfortune of others, I can put the enchantment on you as well, but it's gonna cost ya.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have access to the chest?

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: So, if you have keys from someone else, you're more than welcome to put them in the chest and see what comes out.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: If you want the enchantment that makes the keys, I'm afraid it's gonna cost you 1 million coins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure, I'll pay for that.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Are you sure? 1 million is a lot of coins, and you won't get them back!

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Hmm... I don't think you have enough money on you there, mate.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Ok, let me just... and a bit of... and we're done! You'll now get loot keys whenever you kill someone in the Wilderness. Talk to me again if you have any questions.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Fair enough mate, talk to me again if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not right now, thanks.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Fair enough mate, want to talk about something else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How much?!

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: 1 million coins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do you charge so much?!

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Runes. People don't wanna deliver across the Wilderness, and I'm in no shape to go to them, so I have to pay through the nose.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: I make maybe a couple hundred coins of profit, which at least lets me buy a few beers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why is the beer so cheap if it's so expensive to get deliveries out here?

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: The barman makes it himself with whatever scraps he can get his hands on.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: You'd think it'd turn out horrible, but compared to every other 2 coin beer out there, it's not half bad.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: But enough about beer. If you want access to the loot keys, it's gonna cost you 1 million coins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I change how these loot keys work?

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Well, I can turn the enchantment that creates the loot keys on and off for you. Won't cost you anything to turn it back on again, either.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: If you'd like it so stuff like food and potions stay out of the keys so you can use them yourself, I can do that too.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: And if you like the feeling of picking up valuables, I can make it so that anything above a certain value hits the floor as well.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Ok, whenever you kill someone else now, their items will go to the floor like normal. Just remember, they could have loot keys on them, and they'll still go to your inventory!

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Ok, you'll now get loot keys when you kill another person in the Wilderness.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Ok, food and potions will now be dropped to the floor instead of stored in a loot key.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Ok, food and potions will now be sent to your loot keys.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Ok, valuable items will now be dropped to the floor instead of stored in a loot key.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Ok, valuable items will now be sent to your loot keys.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Currently, if you were to have valuable items drop to the floor, they'd need to be worth at least [number]gp. What do you want to change that value to?

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Hmph. Yeah, real funny, wise guy.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Ok, now items worth at least [entered amount]gp will drop to the floor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How much loot have I claimed?

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Well, seeing as you haven't claimed a key yet, I reckon you've claimed a total of 0gp worth of loot.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: You've claimed 1 key, containing loot worth about [number]gp. I haven't seen you destroy anything of value yet, though.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: You've claimed 1 key, containing loot worth about [number]gp, and you destroyed everything of value.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: You've claimed [number] keys, containing loot worth about [number]gp. I haven't seen you destroy anything of value yet, though.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: You've claimed [number] keys, containing loot worth about [number]gp. You've destroyed [number]gp worth of it.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: You've claimed [number] keys, containing loot worth about [number]gp, and you destroyed everything of value.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you, exactly?

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Ah, used to be an adventurer like you, I guess. Used to roam the world, killing monsters for a living. 'Cept then, I discovered the Wilderness.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Used to take on anyone and everyone who stood in me way, and made myself a small fortune doing it too.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: It's how I got the name, used to go anywhere and everywhere with a skull over my head!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how'd you end up like... this?

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Ah, you live a life feeding on others, eventually something higher up the food chain finds you.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: I used to have a hidden outpost out near the Bone Yard, somewhere to keep my stuff in an emergency. One day all I come back to is a smouldering pile of rubble.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Next thing I know, someone's snuck up behind me and has put a spear through my back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you survived?!

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Always carry a ring of life, friend! Never know when you'll need it.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Still, the damage was done. Everything I ever owned up in smoke, and even though the wound's fully healed, I can still feel that spear in my back to this day.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Thankfully, Ferox was kind enough to take me in, and I managed to repurpose some of my old storage magic for adventurers like you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But why come back to the Wilderness?

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: What, go life a normal life behind a shop counter so I can pedal swamp paste for a living instead? I'll pass!

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Anyhow, enough about me. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: I'll be here if you need anything.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Eyup. They call me Skully. I run this Wilderness Loot Chest. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Well, you know how when you kill someone all of their stuff just ends up in a pile on the floor and it's really hard to pick it all up quickly?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: So, I made an enchantment that teleports the loot to another dimension instead!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How does that help?

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Well, instead of getting a pile of rubbish, you get a single key. You just take the key to this chest and BAM! There's your loot!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So the loot is safe in this other dimension?

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Eh, seems like it. As long as they've had the key, I've never had anyone unable to get the stuff back out. Though if you lose the key, you can't get the stuff out.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Oh, and be careful running about with too many keys on you. Other people will know you've got them, and I'm not gonna stop them opening the chest!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So if I kill someone and take their keys, I can use them myself?

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: ...Eh, if you wanna think about it that way, sure.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: I know you Iron people are weird about taking other people's stuff, so if you just wanna have the chest destroy it all, that's fine by me.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Except the food. I'm eating that instead.

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Now, if you want your own keys instead of relying on the misfortune of others, I can put the enchantment on you as well, but it's gonna cost ya.

sections.title: Interacting with Skully in Deadman Mode

lines.speaker: Skully
lines.text: Sorry mate, can't run the Loot Chest on a Deadman World.
